name: GISENYI
note: Organized killings under military coordination. Tutsi massacred along Lake Kivu shore and in surrounding hills from mid-April.

name: RUHENGERI
note: Killings organized in highland sectors surrounding the Virunga volcanoes. Remote terrain provided brief refuge before systematic searches.

name: BYUMBA
note: Genocide overlapped with RPF military advance from Uganda. Complex violence across northeastern highlands and border zones.

name: KIGALI-VILLE
note: Ground zero. Roadblocks established within hours of April 6. PM Uwilingiyimana killed April 7. 10 Belgian peacekeepers murdered. ETO/Kicukiro: 4,000 abandoned by UNAMIR.

name: KIGALI PREF.
note: Prefecture surrounding the capital. Roadblocks intercepted civilians fleeing Kigali. Systematic house-to-house searches. Kabuga and eastern suburbs targeted early.

name: GITARAMA
note: Interim genocidal government relocated here April 12. Their presence accelerated killings throughout the prefecture. Among the most intense provincial violence.

name: KIBUYE
note: Among the highest proportional death tolls. 12,000+ killed at Gatwaro Stadium. Authorities directed civilians to gather points under false promises of safety.

name: CYANGUGU
note: Systematic killings near DRC border. Bodies disposed of in Lake Kivu. Operation Turquoise (French) established its humanitarian zone partly here in late June.

name: GIKONGORO
note: Site of Murambi Technical School: ~45,000 killed in a single night, April 21. One of the highest per-site death tolls of the genocide.

name: BUTARE
note: Last prefecture to begin. Tutsi prefect Habyarimana (no relation) was removed and killed April 19. Killings then among the most intense. National University targeted.

name: KIBUNGO
note: Nyarubuye: ~20,000 killed at the parish. Bodies thrown into the Kagera River, flowing to Lake Victoria, visible to the world weeks before any intervention.
